articles.title: Chile becomes the first country in the Americas to be verified by WHO for the elimination of leprosy
articles.snippet: The World Health Organization (WHO), together with the Pan American Health Organization (PAHO), congratulates Chile for becoming the first country in the Americas – and the second globally – to be officially verified as having eliminated leprosy disease.

articles.title: Recommendations for influenza vaccine composition for the 2026-2027 northern hemisphere season
articles.snippet: The World Health Organization (WHO) today announced recommendations for the viral composition of influenza (or “flu”) vaccines for the 2026-2027 northern hemisphere influenza season. The announcement was made following a 4-day consultation examining global influenza surveillance data.

articles.title: Denmark becomes first country in the European Union to eliminate mother-to-child transmission of HIV and syphilis
articles.snippet: The World Health Organization (WHO) has certified Denmark for the elimination of mother-to-child transmission (EMTCT) of HIV and syphilis, recognizing the country's sustained commitment to ensuring every child is born free of these infections.

articles.title: Lunar Gateway: Earth’s guard post against asteroids?
articles.snippet: Humankind’s next space outpost, the lunar Gateway, will serve as a staging point to reach the surface of the Moon. A new ESA-backed study is considering whether it could also be used as a deployment point for planetary defence missions, to intercept asteroids approaching dangerously close to Earth.

articles.title: ESA commissions world’s first space debris removal
articles.snippet: ClearSpace-1 will be the first space mission to remove an item of debris from orbit, planned for launch in 2025. The mission is being procured as a service contract with a startup-led commercial consortium, to help establish a new market for in-orbit servicing, as well as debris removal.

articles.title: ESA’s Hera asteroid mission is go

articles.title: Amateur astronomers: help choose asteroid flybys for Hera
articles.snippet: Amateur astronomers around the world are being asked to help the proposed ESA asteroid mission. As well as exploring its final destination – the Didymos binary asteroid system – the Hera spacecraft could potentially fly past one or more bodies on the way. articles.title: Luxembourg firm enabling peek inside asteroid
articles.snippet: ESA’s Juventas is a tiny spacecraft assigned a very big goal. articles.title: Apollo astronaut champions Hera for planetary defence
articles.snippet: Having spent much of the 21st century developing planetary defence techniques, Apollo astronaut Rusty Schweickart is a strong supporter of ESA’s proposed Hera mission. In general, when it comes to asteroid deflection, he says, two spacecraft are better than one.

articles.title: Hera’s radar CubeSat will peer into asteroid’s heart
articles.snippet: ESA’s proposed Hera asteroid mission for planetary defence will carry the smallest radar instrument ever flown in space – flying on a separate mini-spacecraft, it will approach two asteroids closer than its mothership and probe deep into their cores.

articles.title: A legal vacuum on CSAM detection puts children at greater risk

articles.title: Small boats supply chain disrupted: 21 arrested for supplying Channel smugglers with nautical equipment
articles.snippet: A joint investigation conducted under the framework of a Europol task force resulted in a significant disruption of the migrant smuggling supply chain serving criminal networks active along the English Channel. articles.title: Central Mediterranean: 10 migrant smugglers targeted
articles.snippet: On 18 and 19 March 2026, Europol hosted a digital action day under a dedicated Joint Team targeting migrant smugglers operating along the Central Mediterranean route.
